task_completion.description: Generate a multiple sequence alignment of the training data and save it to cytc_aligned.fasta
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE interface showing a multiple sequence alignment of Cytochrome c sequences, or a file explorer/terminal showing 'cytc_aligned.fasta' created in ~/UGENE_Data/hmmer_task/results/.

task_completion.description: Build an HMM profile from the alignment and save it to cytc_domain.hmm
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE HMM build dialog/progress, or the file 'cytc_domain.hmm' visible in the ~/UGENE_Data/hmmer_task/results/ directory.

task_completion.description: Scan the uncharacterized proteome using the generated HMM profile
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE interface showing HMM search results, annotations, or a sequence view of the uncharacterized proteome with identified hits.

task_completion.description: Export the identified sequence annotations to hmm_hits.gff
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE export dialog for annotations, or file explorer/terminal showing 'hmm_hits.gff' present in the results folder.

task_completion.description: Create a discovery report text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open with 'discovery_report.txt' or terminal/file explorer showing its existence in the results folder.

task_completion.description: Document the HMM profile length and the start/end amino acid coordinates of the match in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text editor showing 'discovery_report.txt' containing numeric values explicitly labeled as HMM length (or consensus positions) and start/end coordinates.

task_completion.description: Document the exact FASTA header of the identified homolog in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text editor showing 'discovery_report.txt' containing the FASTA header of the target (must include 'P00056' and 'CYTC_ARATH').

integrity.description: The agent used the UGENE graphical interface to perform the alignment and HMM tasks as requested.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the UGENE GUI being actively used for alignment and HMM scanning, rather than the agent exclusively using command-line tools like 'hmmbuild' or 'hmmsearch' in the terminal.

integrity.description: Output files (.hmm, .gff) were generated by the software, not manually mocked.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of text editors being used to manually type out complex HMM profile matrices or GFF annotation formats.

integrity.description: The data in the discovery report reflects actual findings from the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The coordinates, HMM length, and FASTA header in the text report should logically match the data visible in the UGENE HMM search results screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The uncharacterized proteome contains a synthetic target sequence. The exact FASTA header of the identified homolog must contain the accession 'P00056' and the name 'CYTC_ARATH'. Look for these specific identifiers in the discovery_report.txt.
